methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $78M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
WeaveGrid is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Apoorv Bhargava and John Taggart that has raised $78M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine comparable companies—eight private firms and one public (LNZA)—all operating in the climate and energy sector at similar development stages.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $78M total raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-stage progression, yielding $148M. The peer-median method examines six same-sector, same-stage comparables—Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M)—producing a median valuation of $355M. This peer set ranges from $120M to $5.0B, reflecting meaningful heterogeneity within the frontier-tech climate space.

Triangulating these two approaches yields a valuation range of $126M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $252M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer-median pulls toward the upper range. Key uncertainties center on the appropriate position within this band: Commonwealth Fusion Systems' $5.0B valuation introduces outlier risk, and the scarcity of public comparables limits calibration to later-stage or mature market multiples. WeaveGrid's specific technology performance, capital efficiency, and path to revenue relative to named peers would be critical drivers of positioning within this range.
